message.content:
Test file: src/game-of-life.spec.ts
Implementation file: src/game-of-life.ts
Failing test: "kills the center cell of a fully surrounded 3x3 block (overpopulation)"
Current error: containsCenter expected false but was true; the current rule `countLiveNeighbors(cell) >= 2` doesn't kill cells with more than 3 neighbors.
Passing tests so far: 5

The failing test creates a full 3x3 grid (9 cells) and asserts the center (1,1) is NOT in the next generation output. Center has 8 neighbors → must die (overpopulation, > 3 neighbors).

Change the survival rule from `>= 2` to `>= 2 && <= 3` (or equivalent: alive cell survives with 2 or 3 neighbors).

Verify all existing tests still pass:
- empty -> []
- single cell -> []
- pair -> []
- block (each cell has 3 neighbors) -> survives
- reproduction L-shape -> becomes block (each existing cell has 2 neighbors, so survives with new rule; (1,1) dead with 3 neighbors born)

Do NOT refactor.

EXPERIMENT MODE: Run autonomously, return after completion.

content.text: I'll analyze the failing test and implementation, then make the minimal change needed.

content.text:
All tests pass (6 passing, 2 todo).

Green Phase Complete:
- Implementation: Changed the survivor filter from `>= 2` to `n >= 2 && n <= 3`, so a live cell only survives with exactly 2 or 3 live neighbors.
- Result: All 6 active tests pass (2 todo remain).
- Approach: Minimal change to the survival predicate — the only modification needed to make the overpopulation test pass. The reproduction L-shape test still passes because each of its 3 cells has exactly 2 neighbors (within the survival range), and the dead cell (1,1) has exactly 3 neighbors (gets born).

Relevant file:
- /home/experimenter/experiments/runs/2026-05-16_21-48-45_game-of-life-example-mapping_v7-hybrid-green-refactor_opus-4-7-no-thinking/src/game-of-life.ts

Proceeding to Refactor phase.
